name: Hall 2018
fullDescription: The Hall_2018 dataset was collected in the U.S. and released in 2018. It comprises an average of 8 days of continuous glucose data from partiticipants with no diabetes, prediabetes, and type 2 diabetes. This dataset also contextual information from other data sources including manual user logs of meals, questionnaires, and clinical measurements such as HbA1C and fasting glucose.

data_source.C: Fasting Blood Glucose, Oral Glucose Tolerance Test, Steady-State Plasma Glucose Test, HbA1C, Triglyceride, Cholesterol

name: D1NAMO
fullDescription: The D1NAMO dataset was collected in Switzerland and released in 2018. It comprises as an average of 4 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains contextual  information from other data sources including insulin delivery systems, manual user logs of meals, and wearable tracker data such as electrocardiogram and respiratory data.

name: Colas 2019
fullDescription: The Colas_2019 dataset was collected in Spain and released in 2019. It comprises an average of 2 days of continuous glucose data from participants with no diabetes and type 2 diabetes. This dataset also contains contextual information from clinical and other measurements such as body mass index and HbA1C.

data_source.C: Age, Sex/Gender, BMI, Basal Glycemia, HbA1C, Diabetes Diagnosis

name: OhioT1DM
fullDescription: The OhioT1DM dataset was collected in the U.S. and first released in 2018, followed by a second release in 2020. It comprises an average of 54 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains contextual information from other data sources including insulin delivery systems, wearable trackers, manual user logs, and questionnaires.

data_source.W: Steps, Acceleration, Heart Rate, Skin Temperature, Sleep
data_source.M: Meals, Carbohydrate Estimate, Stressors, Illness, Exercise

name: T1DEXI
fullDescription: The T1DEXI dataset was collected in the U.S. and released in 2022. It comprises an average of 27 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from insulin delivery systems, wearable trackers, manual logging of meals and exercise, and questionnaires.

data_source.I: Insulin pump data, multiple daily injections
data_source.W: Step count, Sleep Metrics, Heart Rate
data_source.Q: Age, Sex, Race, Ethnicity, Weight, Height, Diabetes History

name: T1DEXIP
fullDescription: The T1DEXIP dataset was collected in the U.S. and released in 2022. It comprises an average of 10 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from insulin delivery systems, wearable trackers, manual logging of meals and exercise, and questionnaire data.

data_source.W: Step count, Sleep Metrics, Heart Rate

name: BIGIDEAs
fullDescription: The BIG IDEAs dataset was collected in the U.S. and released in 2023. It comprises an average of 9 days of continuous glucose data from participants with no diabetes and prediabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from wearable trackers, questionnaires, and clinical measurements such as blood pressure and body mass index.

name: DiaTrend
fullDescription: The DiaTrend dataset was collected in the U.S. and released in 2023. It comprises between 31 to 1,907 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from insulin delivery systems, questionnaires, and clinical measurements such as HbA1C.

data_source.I: Insulin pump data (e.g. basal rate, boluses, carbohydrate input)

name: ShanghaiT1DM
fullDescription: The ShanghaiT1DM dataset was collected in China and released in 2023. It comprises between 7 to 42 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from insulin delivery systems, manual user logs of meals, questionnaires, and clinical measurements such as body mass index and HbA1C.

name: ShanghaiT2DM
fullDescription: The ShanghaiT2DM dataset as collected in China and released in 2024. It comprises between 4 to 41 days of glucose data from participants with type 2 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from insulin delivery systems, manual user logs of meals, questionnaires, and clinical measurements such as body mass index and HbA1C.

name: T1DiabetesGranada
fullDescription: The T1DiabetesGranada was collected in Spain and released in 2024. It comprises between 8 to 1,463 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from questionnaires such as age and clinical measurements such as HbA1C.
CGM Device: Abbott FreeStyle Libre 2, Abbott FreeStyle Libre

name: AI-READI
fullDescription: The AI-READI v3.0.0 dataset was collected in the U.S and released in 2025. It comprises an average of 10 days of continuoug glucose data from participants with type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and no diabetes. This dataset also contains contextual information from other data sources including wearable trackers, questionnaires, and clinical/other measurements such as electrocardiograms and retina imaging.

data_source.W: Heart rate, Physical activity, Oxygen saturation, Sleep, Stress
data_source.C: Weight, Height, Blood Pressure, ECG, Retina Imaging, Lab Measurements

name: UCHTT1DM
fullDescription: The UCHTT1DM was collected in Chile and released in 2024. It comprises an average of 6 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from insulin delivery systems, wearable trackers, and manual user logs of meals.

name: HUPA-UCM
fullDescription: The HUPA-UCM was collected in Spain and released in 2024. It comprises between 9 to 575 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from insulin delivery systems, wearable trackers, manual user logs and clinical measurements such as HbA1C.

name: CGMacros Dexcom
fullDescription: The CGMacros_Dexcom was collected in the U.S. and released in 2025. It comprises an average of 11 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and no diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from wearable trackers, manual user logs of meals, questionnaires, and clinical measurements such as HbA1C.

data_source.W: Heart rate, Activity calories, metabolic equivalent of task (Fitbit Sense)
data_source.M: Meal timing, calories, carbs, protein, fat, fiber, meal photo
data_source.C: Weight, Height, HbA1C, fasting glucose, fasting insulin, triglyceride, cholesterol

name: CGMacros Libre
fullDescription: The CGMacros_Libre was collected in the U.S. and released in 2025. It comprises an average of 11 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and no diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from wearable trackers, manual user logs of meals, questionnaires, and clinical measurements such as HbA1C.

name: T1D-UOM
fullDescription: The T1D-UOM was collected in the U.K. and released in 2025. It comprises between 30 to 178 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual information from insulin delivery systems, wearable trackers, manual user logs of meals, questionnaire, and clinical measurements such as HbA1C.

name: Bris-T1D Open
fullDescription: The Bris-T1D dataset was collected in the U.K. and released in 2025. It comprises between 67 to 223 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from insulin delivery systems, wearable trackers, questionnaires and qualitative interviews.
CGM Device: Dexcom G6 / Freestyle Libre 2 / Medtronic Guardian 4 / Dexcom One

data_source.I: Insulin pump data (e.g. basal rate, carbohydrate input)
data_source.W: Heart rate, step count, calories burned
data_source.Q: Age, sex/gender, ethnicity, diabetes duration, qualititive interview and focus group transcripts

name: AZT1D
fullDescription: The AZT1D dataset was collected in the U.S. and released in 2025. It comprises an average of 43 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from insulin delivery systems, questionnaires, and clinical measurements such as HbA1C.

name: Park 2025
fullDescription: The Park_2025 dataset was collected in the U.S. and released in 2025. It comprises continuous glucose data only around oral glucose tolerance tests (~3 hours) each day from participants with no diabetes, prediabetes, and type 2 diabetes, This dataset also contains other contextual data from questionnaires and clinical measurements such as fasting plasma glucose and HbA1C.

data_source.C: BMI, Blood Pressure, Lab measurements (HbA1C, fasting plasma glucose, OGTT, triglyceride, cholesterol, fasting insulin, etc.)

name: PhysioCGM
fullDescription: The PhysioCGM dataset was ollected in the U.S. and released in 2025. It comprises an average of 80 days of continuous glucose data from participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset also contains other contextual data from questionnaires and clinical measurements such as blood pressure and body mass index.
